programme.sub-title: Reggie Yates's Extreme UK: Gay & Under Attack
programme.desc: The first of three programmes exploring the extremes of modern British masculinity. Reggie Yates is shocked to discover that some of the harshest critics - and persecutors - of young black and Asian gays and transsexuals often come from their own communities, or even their own families. He hears about Muslims threatened with exorcism, and West African Christian parents ostracising their own children, and also investigates why so few men of colour are visible at events like Gay Pride, given the cultural isolation they already face(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern try to help the Miller family in North Lincolnshire, with parents Wes and Mel wanting to save up for their wedding after 10 years together. With no savings and big expectations, they need to make considerable changes if they want the wedding of their dreams - can Alex and Steph show these big spenders that it is possible to create memories without breaking the bank, and help them put money aside for their special day? The Great British public gets their say too, as they put four similar-looking biker jackets to the test(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: From the ancient temples of Angkor Wat in Cambodia to the Shaolin Temple in China, discover what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the opening episode, in Cambodia at Angkor Wat, a man who believes the temples are home to his ancestral spirits risks his life to save them from the jungle, while at the Shaolin Temple in China, a Buddhist warrior monk faces a test that could change the course of his life forever(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of the British Garden
programme.desc: The extraordinary changes that occurred in the 19th century, as advances in technology made gardens more lavish, but also more widely available. Monty pays a visit to Queen Victoria's royal retreat at Osborne and Derby's Arboretum, which was the country's first public park. He also investigates the scientific secrets behind the establishment of botanical gardens in Kew and Edinburgh(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: The islanders receive an update on the oil exploration going on offshore. While many residents are excited about the possible benefits such a commodity could bring, others are concerned about the inevitable changes that will occur. Elsewhere, resident marine biologist Megan Tierney pioneers some research using a tracking system, and sets out to catch some Gentoo penguins so that she can find out where they go to feed during the winter. Meanwhile, the islanders practise their dance steps for the annual May Ball(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the gardeners of Marlborough in Wiltshire. They won gold last year and are hoping to retain that coveted award by transforming one of the town's ancient alleyways and also by brightening up a community corner by the river(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the capital on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore London's new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Christy Harrison and Vim Kumaratunga for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Nazan wants her kitchen in Wokingham refreshing on a budget of £3,000, while in Wigan, Chris and Joanna need the balustrade on their staircase replacing(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott takes Len Goodman on a road trip around Britain to expand the Strictly Come Dancing judge's culinary horizons. The pair begin in Manchester, where Len has his first taste of Chinese food and samples some Mexican mezcal, while Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a stir-fried beef dish inspired by the day's adventures(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Mediterranean cuisine and ingredients have influenced British culinary culture since it was first introduced by the Romans, and prepare their favourite recipes(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Farm vet Richard visits a long-standing client, where his work involves anything from de-horning a cow to trimming an overgrown hoof, rounded off with tea and home-made scones. Meanwhile, Megan treats a seriously ill Yorkshire terrier at the Small Animal Hospital in Fife, and there is news for an unsuspecting cat owner when vet Adam makes a surprise discovery. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough documents the universal tale that unites humanity with every other animal on the planet - the journey through life, and the goal to continue the bloodline through offspring. This edition focuses on the first hurdle any animal will face, surviving infancy. Cameras follow flightless barnacle goose chicks as they strive to find food, leaping 400ft down a cliff from the ledge where they hatched. Meanwhile, in New Zealand, young fur seals have stumbled across a safe haven in which they can learn how to avoid predators, while deep in the Gobi Desert, the long-eared jerboa learns to use its impeccable hearing to catch insect pray. Plus, albatross chicks make their first flight, as threatening tiger sharks wait patiently for them to slip up and land in the sea(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Arusha Irvine brave the elements in their search for bargains, heading through the snow from Northumberland to Edinburgh in their '66 Mustang(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts a celebrity edition of the show, as string quartet Bond and composer David Arnold take on the winners of famous game shows for a chance to win money for charity(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. This time, chef Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix to some of his favourite restaurants in Paris. First, the pair visit one of the most famous restaurants in the city, La Tour D'Argent, where they experience a signature dish that has been on the menu since 1890. Moving away from restaurants that offer expensive indulgence, Michel takes Fred to a place reviving one of the simplest and cheapest French classics - the ham baguette. For the final stop of their Parisian trip, the pair visit Brasserie La Couple, which boasts the biggest dining room in Paris(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain demonstrates recipes to take the stress out of impressing guests, including raspberry cheesecake croissants, a speedy satay chicken tray bake and a fuss-free cheat's trifle with creamy chia seeds, yoghurt, fresh fruit and sweet brioche. In Walsall, Nadiya's shortcut curry with spicy jackfruit and speedy naan gives a novice cook the family dinner party that she's always dreamed of(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the third of the student quiz's first-round matches, four students from the University of Birmingham pit their wits against a team from the University of Bristol in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Conjoined Twins: An Impossible Decision
programme.desc: Conjoined twins Marieme and Ndeye have separate hearts and lungs, but Marieme's heart is very weak and her life expectancy is poor and if she dies, her stronger sister will die with her. As the girls' health deteriorates, their father Ibrahima and staff at Great Ormond Street Hospital are forced to decide whether a separation should be considered. This programme tracks the journey of Ibrahima and the medical team as they wrestle with is a seemingly impossible decision with the help of the hospital's Clinical Ethics Committee(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Grace Jones presents a look back at a year when Live Aid took the world by storm, Prince and Bruce Springsteen topped the charts, and Miami Vice set the standard in men's fashion. Anarchic comic Viz hit newsagents' shelves nationwide and the second series of Spitting Image poked fun at the likes of Roy Hattersley and David Steel, who discuss how it felt to be on the receiving end(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: For thousands of years, the Kazakh people of western Mongolia have formed a hunting partnership with golden eagles to help them catch furs to stay warm in the freezing winters. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan joins a family of nomads to learn how they do this, but first he must learn how to train and fly his young golden eagle. Gordon spends days with his bird to build her trust - even letting her sleep in his tent. But it's not just the eagle he has to bond with - he has to get to grips with carrying an eagle on horseback, as well as mucking in with daily chores. Eventually, he faces some of the area's top hunters in a tournament. Will he have made enough progress?(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: Fact-based drama recounting the romantic and creative partnership between influential choreographer-director Bob Fosse and Gwen Verdon, one of the greatest Broadway dancers of all time. The story begins in 1966, mid-way through the couple's marriage, with the launch of the film Sweet Charity, directed by Fosse and based on the successful Broadway musical that had originally starred Verdon. Dining with producer Cy Feuer, Bob puts himself forward as the director of Cy's next project - Cabaret. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: While staying in Majorca, Gwen considers her relationship with Bob, whom she has caught being unfaithful during the filming of Cabaret. She reflects on meeting Fosse years earlier when she had been offered the lead in the musical Damn Yankees when Fosse had insisted on auditioning her. The pair immediately hit it off, placing pressure on Fosse's marriage to the actress Joan McCracken, who was unwell at the time. Bob tells Gwen that he wants to come home, but he is still in love with the German translator Hannah. True-life drama, starring Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The great migration leads to a series of confrontations for the families on the great savannah. Tragedy strikes for lioness Kali, forcing her to make a difficult decision. Bakari challenges the jealous leader of the baboon troop, leading to a violent battle, and elephant mother Nalla has to decide the future of her badly behaved teenager Tembo. Shani the zebra mother has no choice but to cross the river, fully aware a crocodile is waiting - and if he does not get her, the currents might well sweep her away(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Anita Rani head to Gloucestershire to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the Riding for the Disabled Association, attending its national championships at Hartpury College, while Princess Anne talks about her long association with the organisation. Sean Fletcher learns how to make a canoe out of flax, Adam Henson helps with a cherry harvest in Herefordshire and Tom Heap looks at the threat posed by equine flu(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of the British Garden
programme.desc: Monty Don concludes the series by exploring how gardening has developed over the past 100 years, becoming one of Britain's most popular pastimes, with the horticulture industry becoming a key part of Britain's economy. He also reveals the stories of the pioneers who have brought about this gardening revolution(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: As the anniversary of the Battle of Mount Tumbledown arrives, the Rev Richard Hines joins the scouts on a trek up to the summit where they intend to honour those soldiers who died during the fierce fighting of June 1982. They plan to lay a wreath and bury a momento. Meanwhile, the new governor Colin Roberts and his wife Camilla arrive on the island. Elsewhere, Lisa Lowe tends her herd of Ayrshire cows Willow, Ella and Molly, whose fresh milk is like gold dust to Lisa's eager customers in Stanley(n)

programme.title: Dom on the Spot
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins Pendle environmental crime officer Jon on the lookout for litterbugs, while in Romford, bad parking gets drivers a Dom Donut Award(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: The Cullen family have been bringing all the fun of the fair to people's doorsteps since 1860. This documentary looks at this unique way of life and explores whether it is in danger of becoming extinct or can the next generation of Cullens keep the family tradition alive?(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes from their favourite cookery books, explaining their value as social documents that reveal a great deal about people and what they ate. The duo meet Ivan Day, who has one of the most extensive collections of recipe books in the UK, and share stories of renowned food writers such as Elizabeth David and Isabella Beeton(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Adam deals with a life-saving emergency when a dog is hit by a train, and in Teesside, Richard artificially inseminates Welsh black cattle. Meanwhile, at the rural practice Steve tries to clip a budgerigar's claws. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: David Attenborough details the transition animals go through as they grow into adults. Looking at examples including a tiger cub whose family is under threat, a vulnerable veined octopus and a pair of sibling cheetahs guarding against starvation, the veteran naturalist illustrates how they develop new skills and learn how to survive on their own(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It is the second leg with experts James Braxton and Arusha Irvine heading down the west coast from Cumbria to Liverpool, and curious military items are among the desired purchases(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team from Strictly Come Dancing takes on the general knowledge champions. Professionals Robin Windsor, Karen Clifton and Kevin Clifton, 2015 champion Jay McGuiness, People's Strictly winner Cassidy Little and former contestant Anthony Ogogo are the ones hoping to dance circles around the general knowledge champions(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a miniature whisky barrel made from the timbers of a wrecked ship captained by the owner's ancestor, a clockwork train, a set of wicker baby-weighing scales, and a military swagger stick(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a clothing factory in South Shields that manufactures 650 waxed jackets a day. Cherry Healey investigates the science behind waterproof clothing, discovering how a layer of material can keep rain in while allowing sweat to escape, as well as discovering 150-year-old methods of making umbrellas. Plus, Ruth Goodman visits a Scottish harbour to discover the origins of weatherproof coats(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes his group of chefs to Bergen, Norway, where they are challenged to emulate the precise style of well-regarded local chef Christopher Haatuft's award-winning restaurant. The chefs get an in-depth course in the specifics of Scandinavian cooking and ingredients as they prepare venison stew. A more experienced chef joins the brigade to help them rise to the challenge of preparing food for an award-winning restaurant, leaving some of the existing members fearful that they will be replaced(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Olga Koch, Glenn Moore and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian travels to Mongolia, the most sparsely populated country on Earth. With the aid of a local TV host, he spends a week exploring the vast nation. He tries his hand at traditional wrestling and shamanic rituals, encounters an enormous statue of Genghis Khan, takes part in a hunting trip with eagles, and visits an energy centre in the middle of the desert. He also discovers how relationships are formed among nomadic societies, where people often live more than 100 miles away from anyone else(n)

programme.title: Broke
programme.desc: Documentary lifting the lid on people's personal finances, revealing how despite having jobs thousands of families across Britain are struggling to make ends meet. It follows nine families during a critical year as their financial future hangs in the balance. They include a father and son who, after losing their full-time jobs, also lost their house, meaning they now live in tents on the beach, and a former celebrity photographer now working as an Uber driver(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein revisit their investigation into a case of murder and mistaken identity, when a woman's body was found among the sand dunes of Great Yarmouth in 1900(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning visits the Elsecar Heritage Centre in South Yorkshire, where Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Anita also learns about a local man who changed the face of mining in Britain(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle return to Longleat Safari Park, where they get ready to welcome five Southern koalas that are en route from Adelaide in Australia. Jean Johansson is in the wolf wood to see if the European grey wolves can live up to their reputation for having one of the best senses of smell in the animal world(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Hines and vet Steve Pointing limber up for the ultimate Falklands Islands challenge - the mid-winter swim. This means plunging into near freezing Antarctic waters in front of over 200 spectators who have gathered in surf bay to honour this annual tradition. Meanwhile, Dr Becky Edwards contemplates a career change and on HMS Clyde, Captain Sarah Oakley prepares to take part in the commemorations for Liberation Day(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Julian Peedle-Calloo and Yvonne Cobb travel to Reims in France to visit the famous Clin D'Oeil biannual arts festival, attended by thousands of deaf people from around the world(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Piccadilly Circus to Gravesend, visiting an exotic West End store that was popular among Victorian women and hearing how a Russian ballet company took London by storm in 1909. He also discovers the origins of netball and gymslips at North Kent College and meets the pilots of the Port of London Authority, founded in the early 20th century(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke and their guests Simon King and Dallas Campbell(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: South London couple Caroline and James seek design ideas for an alcove storage unit, while Salma from Manchester looks for solutions to remedy the sorry state of her driveway(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len has his first taste of Middle Eastern food and samples Peruvian ceviche in central London. Later, Ainsley takes inspiration from the day and heads to the kitchen to cook chicken shawarma(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers forage for food, exploring Britain's fields and hedgerows in search of free ingredients that will form the basis of spectacular dishes(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Vet Megan treats a Great Dane with a life-threatening condition in Fife, and in the countryside Richard inspects a herd of cows to see how many are pregnant and fits nose rings to some of the bulls. Plus, Steve meets a rabbit with wonky teeth. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: The importance of homes to animals, not only as a place to live but as a shelter from the elements and a refuge from enemies. A pack of African hunting dogs have made their home on a vast plain in Zambia, but it's far from safe as they must battle hyenas and protect their young. Hermit crabs on a tropical island settle down in empty snail shells - and when a new property washes ashore, they all form a queue, then move up the housing chain until all the shells are taken. On the edge of the Sahara are chimpanzees, whose survival relies on knowing where to find water in the most extreme droughts. Here the elders lead a troop on a brutal trek to a dried-out riverbed to dig wells(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and James Braxton scour the shops of Liverpool and Manchester, where James makes a beeline for some tea making equipment, and Roo finds an enormous piece of ceramic(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team comprising children's TV presenter Barney Harwood, former world champion boxer Steve Collins, actor and impressionist Lewis MacLeod, Sky News weather presenter Nazaneen Ghaffar and One Show reporter Alex Riley takes on the general knowledge champions. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team sets to work restoring four treasured family heirlooms. Metal worker Dom Chinea takes on the challenge of restoring an old, dilapidated barber's pole, while luthier David Kennett turns his attention to a broken Portuguese guitar. Elsewhere, Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a Nigerian painting, and Will Kirk transforms a wooden writing slope that hid a life-changing secret(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Nisha Katona takes Fred Sirieix to San Sebastian for a mind-blowing meal at one of the world's top restaurants and to visit a tiny sports bar to learn the secrets of the perfect tortilla. The first place in Nisha's little black book is Mugaritz, ranked as the ninth best restaurant in the world and famous for wild experimentation - something that is made clear as soon as the menus arrive. Instead of a list of dishes to select, the Mugaritz menu is two pictures, one of a swimming fish, one of a flying fish, and Nisha and Fred make their choice, but have little clue what they are going to eat(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In the final episode of the series, the six baby animals approach the end of their first year on Earth. In Iceland, an Arctic fox cub rejected by his mother must learn where to find food for the first time, while in Kenya, spotted hyena twin sisters must roam a territory they share with the biggest threat to their lives, lions. Whether breaking ties or building bonds, all the young animals must learn for themselves how to stay healthy and safe(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam asks Rich's advice when she has an anxiety attack about her new relationship, and Frankie invites her father to dinner(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes his group of chefs to Bergen, Norway, where they get an in-depth course in the specifics of Scandinavian cooking and ingredients as they prepare venison stew. A more experienced chef joins the brigade to help them rise to the challenge of preparing food for an award-winning restaurant, leaving some of the existing members fearful that they will be replaced(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Venice 24/7
programme.desc: Artists arrive for the Biennale arts festival, but the grand scale of some of the exhibits puts a strain on the city's infrastructure. Anish Kapoor tries to stage his installation Ascension at the Basilica di San Giorgio, while another artist struggles to transport a four-ton sculpture by canal. The Fenice theatre undergoes a vital safety check, and a cruise liner sails dangerously close to the city centre(n)

programme.title: Arena: Philip Hoare's Guide to Whales
programme.desc: Acclaimed author and whale-watcher Philip Hoare explores the worlds of three types of whale, beginning with baleen whales, the largest animals ever to have lived(n)

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster joins a team of historians and geologists to recreate a pioneering 280-mile journey through the natural wonder that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Following in Powell's footsteps in the stunning yet challenging north Arizona landscape, Snow and the crew take on the area's rapids in antique wooden boats as they learn about the region's geological and Native American past(n)

programme.title: James May's Toy Stories
programme.sub-title: James May's Toy Stories: Action Man
programme.desc: For decades, Action Man has promised much with its eagle eyes, gripping hands and array of outfits, but what has he ever achieved? James May has decided he is going to try to redeem its reputation by propelling one of the figures through the sound barrier and return it safely to Earth. James and his team have to deal with high-speed physics, unforeseen explosions and catastrophic design setbacks, before settling on an experimental supersonic vehicle made entirely from scratch. Finally, Action Man must go faster and higher than another attempt led by arch-rival Sindy(n)

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: An insight into mammals' ability to learn new tricks to aid hunting prey and evading predators. Slow-motion cameras reveal the star-nosed mole's technique for detecting its next meal underwater, while an ibex runs up a steep cliff face to escape a fox. A killer whale off the coast of the Falklands sneaks into a pool used by elephant seal pups and snatches them, saving itself the trouble of searching for food in the open sea, and three cheetah brothers work together to hunt a zebra. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Pedalling Dreams: The Raleigh Story
programme.desc: Documentary telling the tale of the ups and downs of Raleigh bikes, using personal testimony and rare and previously unseen archive film. Born in the back streets of Nottingham in 1888, Raleigh grew to become the biggest bicycle manufacturer in the world and, for more than a century, was known for its simple and practical bikes(n)

programme.title: Cornwall's Native Poet: Charles Causley
programme.desc: Profile of the poet, who lived his entire life in the Cornish town of Launceston with his mother, and wrote verse that documented the changes in the town through two world wars, as well as his thoughts on local history, mythology, animals and faith(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Flights of Fancy: Pigeons and the British
programme.desc: Cameras venture inside places of sporting achievement, scientific endeavour and male obsession - the lofts of pigeon fanciers - to tell the story of a remarkable bird. As racer, messenger and even beauty pageant contestant, the humble pigeon has been a steadfast part of British life for centuries(n)

programme.title: The Art of Spain
programme.desc: Art historian Andrew Graham-Dixon travels into the heart of Spain, exploring Madrid and its surrounding provinces. He looks at the correlation between the powerful 16th and 17th-century Spanish Empire - fuelled by an increasingly fervent form of Catholicism - and the reaction of the artists, including El Greco and Ribera, whose work created and defined the golden age of Spanish painting(n)

programme.title: Africa: A Journey into Music
programme.desc: Rita Ray heads to South Africa, home to distinctive vocal harmonies that have travelled all over the world. Visiting Johannesburg, Durban and Cape Town, she discovers the songs and harmonies that have given the country a voice abroad. Rita meets social campaigner Denis Goldberg, who was imprisoned as part of the anti-apartheid movement, Sipho Mabuse, a giant of the nation's music, Cape Town rapper Kanyi Mavi, singer and innovator Thandiswa Mazwai and Madosini Latozi Mpahleni, aka Madosini - a cultural icon who has performed at Womad festival(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Venice 24/7
programme.desc: The canal system is closed to ordinary traffic for a day as the city celebrates the ancient tradition of rowing, with fleets of oar-powered boats taking to the water. Police clamp down on speeding motor boats, while on the outskirts of Venice work begins on the installation of an anti-flood system that could prevent the city from being submerged by rising waters(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre examines how continued success for the unit could only be achieved with ever more dangerous and ambitious missions, while David Stirling's new second-in-command Captain Paddy Mayne was an officer as unpredictable and dangerous as the phase of war that was about to begin. The historian also reveals how many SAS members were captured by the Germans - and hears the extraordinary story of two of them who outran their pursuers by surviving for three days in the desert without food or fresh water(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili presents a look at the history of rocket technology, from its origins in ancient China, where a monk created a bamboo projectile loaded with homemade explosive concocted from bat droppings. The programme examines how a breakthrough in dairy farming influenced the development of space flight, and reveals the science behind modern ion drives, which run on solar power and make inter-planetary travel a possibility(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of the Voyager probes, launched in 1977 and beaming back information across unfathomable distances - and in the case of Voyager 1, now reaching the void of deep space(n)

programme.title: The Queen Mary: Greatest Ocean Liner
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the Clyde-built ship, from the maiden voyage in May 1936 to New York, and her 1939 role in the Battle of the Atlantic, to her current status as a floating luxury hotel and museum in Long Beach, California. Among the contributors are singer Johnny Mathis(n)

programme.title: Archaeology: A Secret History
programme.desc: Richard Miles charts the history of archaeological breakthroughs in a mission to understand the ancient past. In the first programme, he explores how the profession began by trying to prove a biblical truth(n)

programme.title: The Culture Show
programme.sub-title: The Culture Show: Wars of the Heart
programme.desc: For those who remained in London during the Second World War, the Blitz was a time of sleeplessness, fear and loss, but some of the city's literary set found inspiration in the danger and intensity. James Runcie examines how novelists Graham Greene, Henry Green and Elizabeth Bowen and poet Hilda Doolittle revelled in the creative and personal freedom, and reveals how these writers distilled the surreal and often frightening atmosphere of the time into some of their finest work(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre examines how continued success for the unit could only be achieved with ever more dangerous and ambitious missions, while David Stirling's new second-in-command Captain Paddy Mayne was an officer as unpredictable and dangerous as the phase of war that was about to begin. The historian also reveals how many SAS members were captured by the Germans - and hears the extraordinary story of two of them who outran their pursuers by surviving for three days in the desert without food or fresh water(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili presents a look at the history of rocket technology, from its origins in ancient China, where a monk created a bamboo projectile loaded with homemade explosive concocted from bat droppings. The programme examines how a breakthrough in dairy farming influenced the development of space flight, and reveals the science behind modern ion drives, which run on solar power and make inter-planetary travel a possibility(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Venice 24/7
programme.desc: The emergency services monitor a religious festival that is being celebrated with a rave, leaving inebriated party-goers at risk of falling into the city's famous canals. Plus, paramedics struggle to reach an unconscious reveller, argumentative drinkers cause a problem, and a firework display brings the celebrations to a close with a bang(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Archaeologist Neil Oliver explores more than 2,000 years of Scottish history, casting a fresh perspective on the events and characters of the country's past against a backdrop of stunning scenery and iconic landmarks. He begins by examining identity, starting in the first century AD, when the tribes of a place the Romans called Caledonia banded together to confront the legions of the Empire(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley examines how George II had to adapt to a growing middling rank in society - who were no longer content with being subjects. Affairs of the state began to be openly discussed in coffee houses, and the king and his ministers were mocked in satire - with George becoming an easy target due to his frequent absences. His wife Caroline went on to popularise a medical breakthrough against smallpox, but it was their son Frederick Prince of Wales who had the popularity the monarchy needed to survive in the modern era(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Ultimate Rome: Empire Without Limit
programme.desc: Scholar Mary Beard takes an in-depth look at identity and citizenship within the Roman Empire, including what it meant to be, or become, Roman, and how the different parts of the Empire reacted to Roman rule. She follows the story of an African Roman from his native land to Britain, where he served as a governor, and in York and Newcastle she finds the remains of Romans, but not as one might imagine(n)

programme.title: Akala's Odyssey
programme.desc: The rapper and poet explores the world of Homer and the Odyssey, to discover how an epic poem became the cornerstone of Western literature and how his own experiences have been impacted by a 3,000-year-old classic. Akala travels on a personal journey from the ruins of Troy to the high city of the Acropolis, looking at how language can create national identity and how the Odyssey endures today(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Buster tries to convince his friends that there is such thing as a Megatoad, but his story leads him to challenge his own beliefs(n)

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.sub-title: Today the Dog, Tomorrow the World
programme.desc: Power-hungry tiny alien, Empress Miasma, takes control of Gnasher from the inside and uses him in her attempts to take over planet Earth(n)

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are celebrating another noisy victory, gleefully ringing Big Ben's bell when an over-excited Penfold falls, pushing the clock hand forward, causing the sun to go down prematurely. Unbeknownst to them a mysterious figure watches on from nearby noting the clock's incredible time-controlling powers. When DM realises that the day is being stolen and replaced with a silent night, he enlists the help of expert colleagues - the night shift! With Danger Moth and Danger Mole in tow the team track the quiet culprit. But a noisy Danger Mouse might just be outwitted by this stealthy saboteur(n)

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience(n)

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. Oona tells the story of when she switched bodies with a fly.(n)

programme.title: The Deep
programme.desc: The Nektons are trying to identify a unique life form. They've never seen it and have only heard its sound - bloop. Just as Ant discovers its true nature, a huge underwater mining machine threatens to destroy it(n)

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience(n)

programme.title: Dragons: Race to the Edge
programme.desc: When Dagur appears at the Edge claiming to know where Viggo keeps his gold, the Dragon Riders fly off to find the stash and return Berk's stolen gold. Flashing images(n)

programme.title: The Zoo
programme.desc: The zoo stinks. All fingers are pointed at the tapirs - who are prone to plopping in the pool - but can they stem the stench before the pubic arrives?(n)

programme.title: The Pets Factor
programme.desc: Cheryl operates on a dog with a broken leg, Rory treats a cat with some serious swelling on his side and James has a visit from a litter of basset hound pups(n)

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The first five quarter-finalists enter the tent hoping for a coveted place in the semis, but first they must create a choux pastry ring and a decorative fruit pie(n)

programme.title: Millie Inbetween
programme.desc: Millie is now a teenager and has her first crush, while Craig gets a Saturday job, Lauren takes a hike and Dad is not best pleased when Amber's ex turns up(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Phoenix FC have a new home and a new coach. But can Jamie adapt to his new circumstances, prove himself as captain — and cope with life without his best friend Jack?(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Hoping that Cup glory can convince Jack to return, Jamie seeks to impress Molly(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: The U14s need a new goalkeeper, while the U12s just need to get their act together(n)

programme.desc: Drama about a gifted schoolboy footballer, whose parents have recently split forcing him to move house and school. Louis Dunn stars(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Dillon and Liam assert themselves in different ways. Will they stand up for what's right?(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Zoe rises to a new challenge, but how will she react when her dream is derailed?(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Troubled Eric refuses to play in the U12's next game. Can Molly uncover his secret?(n)

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Louis Dunn is in his home town of Manchester to meet Idris, a young amputee footballer with big dreams. Spotted at school by a disability coach from Manchester City, Idris now plays every week with a community team. Idris has his eye on a more competitive future, and is hoping to make it to the final training session for the England Amputee team before the World Cup in Mexico(n)

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Part two of two. Louis Dunn travels to meet Idris at the final England Amputee Football training camp before the World Cup in Mexico. The pair meet England and Manchester City star striker Jamie Tregaskiss, who is an inspiration for Idris and an example of where he could be one day if he keeps up his hard work on the pitch(n)

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Historical characters and events are explored in sketches and quizzes based on the books by Terry Deary. Comedy talent including Sarah Hadland, Steve Punt and Meera Syal help bring the strange facts, gory battles and crazy inventions to life(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Drama about a gifted schoolboy footballer, whose parents have recently split forcing him to move house and school. Louis Dunn stars(n)

programme.title: The Zoo
programme.desc: Naomi the grey gibbon and Gambira the orang-utan share an island, but famously cannot get along. When Naomi mysteriously disappears, Gambira is delighted - until Jurgen the gorilla wants to move in. Narrated by Hugh Dennis(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Bitzer gets his wish for some peace and quiet when Shaun becomes hypnotised by Mowermouth the goat(n)

programme.desc: The Mushrooms have their photo taken, and Stunt Tomato tries another trick. Meanwhile, Cheeky Cheese sets a trap for Strongman Monkey Nut(n)

programme.desc: Walter launches his own mega-dull, hypnotic TV channel with an signal booster that swamps all other channels(n)

programme.title: Diddy TV
programme.desc: Diddy Dom explores his past on a pogo stick in Great Pogo Stick Journeys and will someone win the jackpot in TV's easiest game show, The Answer is Always Sausage?(n)

programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience(n)

programme.desc: Sarah turns up looking a little off colour whilst Ricky makes roar-some dragon feet for your trainers and the Grand Master makes rude sounding slime that doesn't leave a smell(n)

programme.title: The Pets Factor
programme.desc: Cheryl operates on a dog with a broken leg, Rory treats a cat with some serious swelling on his side and James has a visit from a litter of basset hound pups(n)

programme.desc: The school's newest students are given a chance to choose their cat familiars, but Mildred is dismayed to learn all the black cats are gone, and she is left with a timid tabby(n)

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve goes on a mission to track down the deadliest of all dinosaur defenders. He meets the armored tank that is Nodosaurus, tests whether an Iguanodon really has what it takes to beat a T-Rex, and - thanks to some mega engineering - unleashes an Ankylosaurus tail-swinging robot that goes on the rampage(n)

programme.title: HH: Gory Games
programme.desc: Return of the historical game show, hosted by Dave Lamb and his assistant Rattus Rattus from Horrible Histories. In the first episode, Olivia, Aaron and Maya battle it out to win Year Spheres in games including Wart Are You Looking At and Feeling Eel(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield look back at all the amazing dens built throughout the series and reminisce the big reveals(n)

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A selection of songs from the fourth series, including the Pilgrim Fathers rapping about Jamestown, Mary Seacole singing about Crimea and William Shakespeare with his big band(n)

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The presenter goes on the hunt for the most deadly animals to have ever walked the earth(n)

programme.title: Shark Bites
programme.desc: Steve Backshall meets the most ravenous shark in the sea - the tiger shark. Known for eating tin cans and even car number plates, these sharks are in fact perfectly honed hunters(n)

programme.title: The Worst Witch

programme.desc: The first of three programmes exploring the extremes of modern British masculinity. programme.desc: Roller derby is one of the fastest growing sports in Europe. This documentary reveals the lives and loves of Wales' first and best roller derby team Tiger Bay Brawlers, following them through the biggest moment of their sporting lives - the European Continental Championships. programme.desc: Jenny Eclair narrates a documentary about Norfolk artist Ruddy Muddy, aka Rick Minns, who uses mud to create paintings on the side of his van and has raised thousands of pounds for local charities(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The residents are rocked by the news of Phil's attack, although some who know a little more than others begin to panic and try to cover the truth. Eventually, however, an arrest is made. But have the police got the real culprit? A mix-up over annoying loud music leaves Ruby racked with guilt, and inadvertently leads to an argument between Linda and Mick. Sheree makes herself a little too much at home, much to Denise's annoyance, and Callum struggles to write his wedding vows(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Stacey Meets the IS Brides - Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley travels to holding camps in Kurdish-controlled northern Syria, where she meets Western women who left their home countries to join Islamic State, and also finds the camps filled with European children. The Kurds in control of this part of Syria say the Western powers should not expect them to be jailers on their behalf indefinitely, and Stacey explores the question of what to do with these women and children who have been left behind(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In a first for the series, two related celebrities join forces to investigate their family tree as comedian and actor Jack Whitehall and his father and occasional double-act partner and talk show co-host Michael team up to look into their past. They learn about a tragedy that left Michael's grandfather an orphan, and also follow their lineage back to Wales in the 1830s, where they find out about their Tory ancestor and his role fighting against the Chartist political and social reform movement(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: White Van Gogh Man - Our Lives
programme.desc: Jenny Eclair narrates a documentary about Norfolk artist Ruddy Muddy, aka Rick Minns, who uses mud to create paintings on the side of his van and has raised thousands of pounds for local charities(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The residents are rocked by the news of Phil's attack, although some who know a little more than others begin to panic and try to cover the truth. Eventually, however, an arrest is made. But have the police got the real culprit? A mix-up over annoying loud music leaves Ruby racked with guilt, and inadvertently leads to an argument between Linda and Mick. Sheree makes herself a little too much at home, much to Denise's annoyance, and Callum struggles to write his wedding vows(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Stacey Meets the IS Brides - Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley travels to holding camps in Kurdish-controlled northern Syria, where she meets Western women who left their home countries to join Islamic State, and also finds the camps filled with European children. The Kurds in control of this part of Syria say the Western powers should not expect them to be jailers on their behalf indefinitely, and Stacey explores the question of what to do with these women and children who have been left behind(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In a first for the series, two related celebrities join forces to investigate their family tree as comedian and actor Jack Whitehall and his father and occasional double-act partner and talk show co-host Michael team up to look into their past. They learn about a tragedy that left Michael's grandfather an orphan, and also follow their lineage back to Wales in the 1830s, where they find out about their Tory ancestor and his role fighting against the Chartist political and social reform movement(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates's Extreme UK
programme.sub-title: Reggie Yates's Extreme UK: Gay & Under Attack
programme.desc: The first of three programmes exploring the extremes of modern British masculinity. Reggie Yates is shocked to discover that some of the harshest critics - and persecutors - of young black and Asian gays and transsexuals often come from their own communities, or even their own families. He hears about Muslims threatened with exorcism, and West African Christian parents ostracising their own children, and also investigates why so few men of colour are visible at events like Gay Pride, given the cultural isolation they already face(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern try to help the Miller family in North Lincolnshire, with parents Wes and Mel wanting to save up for their wedding after 10 years together. With no savings and big expectations, they need to make considerable changes if they want the wedding of their dreams - can Alex and Steph show these big spenders that it is possible to create memories without breaking the bank, and help them put money aside for their special day? The Great British public gets their say too, as they put four similar-looking biker jackets to the test(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: From the ancient temples of Angkor Wat in Cambodia to the Shaolin Temple in China, discover what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the opening episode, in Cambodia at Angkor Wat, a man who believes the temples are home to his ancestral spirits risks his life to save them from the jungle, while at the Shaolin Temple in China, a Buddhist warrior monk faces a test that could change the course of his life forever(n)

programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.sub-title: Ross Kemp: Living with Young Carers
programme.desc: It is believed that one in 20 children in Britain care for sick parents or siblings. Ross Kemp meets some of these young carers, including a 15-year-old who looks after her schizophrenic mother, and an even younger boy who cares for his sister, who has cerebral palsy and visual impairment. Ross finds out how much money these young carers are saving the economy, but also considers how funding to help them is failing(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Now Murtaugh has met his match in the shape of former international CIA agent Wesley Cole, the newly melded partners attempt to find a steady rhythm as they work to solve the murder of a finance executive. Cole, who has returned home to assumes duty as an LAPD officer, also has to navigate his role with his ex and his daughter. Meanwhile, Avery grapples with the outcome of his campaign for city council. Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Kevin Rahm star in the fast-paced detective drama based on the hit feature films(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Part one of two. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade takes on the biggest investigation of his career, hoping to unravel the exact fate of ocean liner RMS Laconia, which after being converted into an armed merchant cruiser was torpedoed by a German U-boat in the mid-Atlantic in September 1942. At the time of the attack the vessel was carrying a mixture of British soldiers, Polish soldiers, Italian prisoners of war and civilians, 1,500 of whom died. This investigation leads Jeremy to the largest fish he had ever caught, as well as the deepest place he had ever been(n)

programme.desc: The unlikely decades-long friendship between prison officer ‘Jonesy' and repeat offender burglar ‘Jonno', who are about to go their separate ways, in retirement and release to the outside world respectively. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Eileen is gutted when Jan tells her that he's a liar and a cheat and that he never cared for her. Meanwhile, Gary confronts Ryan over his apparent windfall, and Claudia announces that she is moving into her flat after all(n)

programme.title: The Widower
programme.desc: DS Henry raids Malcolm's home after hearing he's embezzling funds at an angling club, and finds stolen laptops as well as an unlicensed firearm - but legal technicalities result in the case being dropped. The detective then discovers Malcolm and Simone are planning a transatlantic trip and fears the killer could use this opportunity to murder his fiancee. Will he be able to warn her before it's too late? Fact-based drama, starring Reece Shearsmith, John Hannah and Archie Panjabi(n)

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Homestead Correctional Institution in Florida to meet Amber Wright, who, at the age of 15 conspired with a group of friends to murder her ex-boyfriend Seath Jackson, an innocent teenage boy who was beaten, shot, and knee-capped before his corpse was burnt and his remains thrown in a quarry. Later, Piers travels to meet Seath's parents in Summerfield, Florida, with their visceral anger standing in stark contrast to Amber's cool demeanour. It soon emerges that Amber was a central player in the plot to kill Seath, but will she take responsibility for her actions?(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow the stories of three families who have faced challenges building their new relationships. Brenda Rhensius's daughter Joanne has struggled with the new situation because of the loyalty she feels towards her adoptive mother, while Spencer Brown and his sister Margot have been gradually trying to bring their two worlds together. The third reunion was not originally shown and it is only three years that Hazel Stubbs Races and her son Kenneth feel ready to share their experience.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar as detectives investigating a cold-case killing from almost 40 years earlier. The discovery of a skeleton in the cellar of a demolished building launches a murder hunt, headed up by DCI Cassie Stuart and DS Sunny Khan. However, all they have to go on is a car key found near the body, which leads them on a search to find out who he is, as well as how, when and why he died. WithTom Courtenay, Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill, Ruth Sheen, Gemma Jones and Hannah Gordon(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Having identified the body in the cellar as Jimmy Sullivan, Cassie visits the victim's mother, who is relieved that she might finally find out what happened to her son 39 years after he went missing. The detectives focus their investigation on the names in Jimmy's diary, who are revealed to be a government advisor, a priest, a retired bookkeeper and a housewife with a secret past. What part, if any, could these four wildly different people have played in Jimmy's disappearance and demise? Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar star in the crime drama, with Tom Courtenay, Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill and Ruth Sheen(n)

programme.desc: Terry takes a job at a strip club, while a chance meeting with an old acquaintance gives Arthur an idea to make money out of art forgeries. Vintage comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman and George Cole(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is approached by a woman desperate to prove her imprisoned father is innocent of murder. As he proceeds, the case causes him to become plagued by doubt and believe he sent an innocent man to jail for the murder of his former partner. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star(n)

credits.actor: Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins, Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A trusted informant tips Regan off about an upcoming robbery, but the gang involved kidnaps his daughter to blackmail him into taking no action. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Rachel, Cameron, Claire and David work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Discover how the famous Henry vacuum cleaner is made, and go behind the scenes at the family firm churning out dairy ice cream. Narrated by Jimmy Nail(n)

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: A ban is placed on alcohol in Benidorm due to a religious festival, but the guests are determined to ignore it, despite the staff trying their best to enforce the new rules. Meanwhile, while Joyce is away, Sam sees another opportunity to make a few extra euros, and Pauline's mysterious Malcolm arrives and she seems to be under his spell yet again(n)

programme.title: Pearl Harbor
programme.desc: The reported death of a pilot during the Second World War leaves his girlfriend seeking solace in the arms of his one-time best friend - until the supposedly dead airman turns up. However, when the Japanese launch a bombing raid on a US base in Hawaii, the three of them have to put aside their personal problems. Romantic drama, starring Ben Affleck, Josh Hartnett, Kate Beckinsale and Cuba Gooding Jr(n)

credits.actor: Ben Affleck, Josh Hartnett, Kate Beckinsale, Cuba Gooding Jr

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of previous investigations from the programme delving into the secrets behind mass-produced food. Jimmy Doherty heads to Kenya to find out what makes one tea more expensive than another, before meeting a taster for one of the UK's largest tea producers. Matt Tebbutt braves the icy waters of the North Sea to join divers harvesting scallops by hand from the sea bed, while Kate Quilton is in Israel to learn why some limes are much juicier than others(n)

programme.title: The £1 Houses: Britain's Cheapest Street
programme.desc: Ibby has just days to go until her council inspection, but without a fitted kitchen and a long list of other jobs still for builder Yousef to complete, will she achieve her dream of finally owning her own home? John is left to try and finish his house on his own when Rob goes on holiday to France, moving day has finally arrived for Debbie and Ste, and Mel and Rob finally see progress on their street(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: The latest incident in a knife crime epidemic sees officers from Bedfordshire Police respond to a 999 call reporting a stabbing in broad daylight. Cameras follow detectives as they race to the scene, where a young man lies bleeding on the ground, and every twist and turn of the investigation is followed in scenes that have become all too familiar across the country. This is real-life drama where the focus on the human story behind the statistics is more compelling than the seemingly endless daily headlines(n)

programme.title: Born Famous
programme.desc: In an exploration of social mobility in the UK, four people who are the children of celebrities live in their parents' old communities to find out what sort of life they might have had if their mothers or fathers not become hugely successful in their chosen fields. In the first edition, Gordon Ramsay's son Jack heads to the Bretch Hill estate in Banbury, Oxfordshire, to discover what life could have been like had his dad Gordon not become a world-famous chef(n)

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.sub-title: Homes from Hell: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Real-life footage reveals some of the dangers lurking inside homes, from bugs and the chaos caused by animals and children to dodgy DIY and conflict with neighbours. Psychologists also reflect on the anxieties that can be caused for homeowners experiencing these situations while trying to relax(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Council House Scandal
programme.desc: The architect embarks on a campaign to kick start a housing revolution, and attempts to uncover the reasons behind the steep decline in affordable council house building. George meets visionary architects of the past, and visits the best and worst examples of housing on offer, and a trip to Vienna - where social housing can come with indoor and outdoor pools - proves inspirational for his housing vision for the future. Back in the UK, George challenges government to reform housing policy, before taking matters into his own hands on his campaign to kick start a new wave of council house building(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business(n)

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast

Jamie and Jimmy take a closer look at nutritional training for medical students, as they campaign to give our future doctors the tools to tackle 21st-century health conditions. With a brief set by the garden's curator, they need to take their designs to the next level if they want to win. Meanwhile, Jennie Bond is in Somerset to find an impressive Wellington Monument to commemorate his victory at the battle of Waterloo, Suzannah Lipscomb travels to Hawkers Hut in Morwenstow, Cornwall, the bolt hole for another eccentric character and Shappi Khorsandi is at Sissinghurst Castle, Kent, to tell the story of another love that could not be(n)

programme.title: The Murder of Charlene Downes
programme.sub-title: Vanished: The Murder of Charlene Downes
programme.desc: On November 1, 2003, 14-year-old Charlene Downes left her home in Blackpool and was never seen again. This documentary examines the events that followed. A wall of lies and misinformation hindered the subsequent investigation into Charlene's disappearance, until one man stepped forward. The information this witness gave to the police unveiled what was considered to be Britain's first grooming-gang scandal. It also enabled Lancashire Police to launch its biggest-ever investigation, including secret surveillance of two new suspects(n)

programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gives another of the nation's favourite dishes a twist, demonstrating how to make a marshmallow pavlova(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty finds out if eating too many poppy seeds could make him fail a drugs test, Kate Quilton learns the consequences of tipping waste cooking oil down the drain, and Matt Tebbutt has a Sicilian bake-off with an increasingly popular ancient grain bread(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel redesigns the rooms in one of the chateau's turrets with a fruits and flowers motif - but an unexpected surprise lurks behind her creations. Outside, Dick is putting together a parkour fitness trail in the 12 acres of grounds, but hits upon an unexpected obstacle buried deep underground(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers are tested to the limit in pastry week, including an ambitious final challenge that sees them pushed to the edge creating a majestic pie fit for a banquet. Before that, the Signature challenge centres on samosas, with a sweet and a savoury option required, and there is a classic French Technical involving two types of pastry. Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith decide which of the bakers has to leave the competition, with Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig on hand for some emotional support and encouragement(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Henry Cole visit a motorbike fan, making table lamps from engines and turning a pallet and iron wheels into a stylish modern coffee table(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the King's Head in Addlethorpe, Lincolnshire, where landlady Helen Coy and her bar manager and best friend Emma Green are pulling out all the stops to win. The B&B owners are taken back to their childhood at the funfair, At dinner, B&B beginners Louise and Andy Macbeth rattle nerves when Louise reveals her past career as a hospitality journalist - leaving pub veteran Helen less than impressed(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: B&B newcomers Andy and Louise Macbeth invite their rival hoteliers to spend the night at the dog-friendly Beck Hall in North Yorkshire, where an extra-critical Helen and Emma discover what they believe to be stray dog hairs in their bed. A fishing trip makes Terri feel a little out of sorts, and when the time arrives for feedback, the managers are surprised to learn two of their guests have indicated they would not come back for a second stay(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Albaston in Torquay for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed by owners and avid souvenir collectors, Kevin and Julie Shadbolt. Terri and Stuart are impressed by their room, but are startled by the memorabilia in the communal lounge, and expectations are raised at dinner when Kevin reveals he has a secret recipe for success when it comes to breakfast. However, at feedback the next day, tensions begin to rise(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay of the week is in Bournemouth, at Terri and Stewart's specialist dance holiday venue the Hotel Piccadilly, where a class in the grand ballroom soon has the guests in a spin. However, temperatures rise over dinner when the simmering tension between Louise and Helen finally boils over(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet up for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. King's Head landlady Helen challenges Louise and Andy about the problems they reported on cleanliness in the bathroom. while Louise and Andy from Beck Hall get hot under the collar when the others criticise their dog-friendly approach(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer guide prospective buyers through the complex world of property viewings and agents. They begin in the popular commuter county of Kent, where homes are snapped up quickly due to their proximity to London. Robin and Gemma have recently returned to the UK after three years in Singapore and want a family home to bring up their baby, while in Tunbridge Wells, Gemma and Barney are looking to buy their first place together in a very competitive market(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Andrew Winter and Neale Whitaker help a family of nine in Canterbury, New South Wales, decide whether to renovate their current bungalow or look for a new home(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: African painted dogs K'mana and Ville were thrown together as a breeding couple after the dominant female died, but K'mana was never Ville's first choice as partner and still carries a limp from her battles with Ville's previous mate. Now she is pregnant with the zoo's first ever litter of this rare and endangered species. Meanwhile, keepers try to rekindle interest between Okapi pair Dicky and Stuma, and Atlas moths Dave and Simon only have five days of life after hatching, and their sole purpose is to mate(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A two-year old mastiff with a bone tumour in its leg undergoes pioneering surgery, only to return a few weeks later suffering pain in its foot. A tiny pug faces the prospect of euthanasia after losing control of its hind legs, but neurologist Colin Driver thinks spinal surgery could give hope, and a Rhodesian ridgeback is brought in suffering from lameness. Surgery seems like the only option, but Noel and the owners are concerned whether this is the right course of action in the long run(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Fresh from the success of hosting a three-day wedding at the chateau, Dick and Angel make business plans for the grounds, intending to charge people to fish in the moat and forage in the woods. Angel starts work on a second guest suite, but also wants to install an elevator, which pushes Dick's engineering skills to the limit(n)

programme.title: Race to the Death: Rome's Charioteers
programme.desc: Documentary exploring new discoveries about chariot racing in ancient Rome, exploring how influential the sport was in Roman culture. While charioteers are still among some of the best-paid sports stars in history, the stakes were particularly high, with riots provoked by the results of races and curses employed to influence the outcome. The documentary follows a team attempting to reconstruct a racing chariot and take it round a test track, and uncovers the story of the slave who became Rome's most successful driver(n)

programme.title: Living in the Shadow of World War Two
programme.desc: A look at the importance of maintaining the morale of the civilian population, with examples from radio and cinema that helped to lighten the darkness(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-one-year-old Mark is brought in after coming off his bike and doctors are concerned he may have suffered life-changing damage to his spine, with wife Karen rushing to be by his side. Ten-year-old Aiden comes to paediatrics with his mum and her girlfriend Lorraine, after hurting his foot on the monkey bars at school(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: A dramatic 911 call reveals that a helicopter crash has taken place as wealthy entrepreneur Charles Burnett III and his friends fly to his ranch in New Mexico(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A two-year old mastiff with a bone tumour in its leg undergoes pioneering surgery, only to return a few weeks later suffering pain in its foot. A tiny pug faces the prospect of euthanasia after losing control of its hind legs, but neurologist Colin Driver thinks spinal surgery could give hope, and a Rhodesian ridgeback is brought in suffering from lameness. Surgery seems like the only option, but Noel and the owners are concerned whether this is the right course of action in the long run(n)
